steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The blank describes MPB's relationship to estuary ecosystems. The decisive clue is the second sentence: decreased MPB abundance “has a direct, negative impact on coastal food webs.” If losing MPB directly damages ecosystems, then MPB must be essential — not merely representative, secondary, or replaceable.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘imperative for’: “Imperative” means absolutely necessary. This fits: if removing MPB directly harms the food web, they are essential to it. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘emblematic of’: “Emblematic” means symbolically representative. A symbol can be removed without damaging the ecosystem; the passage describes real, direct harm, not symbolic significance. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘subordinate to’: “Subordinate” means lower in rank or importance. The passage shows MPB are foundational to the food web, not secondary to it. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘interchangeable with’: “Interchangeable” means replaceable. If MPB were replaceable, losing them would not cause direct negative impact; the passage says the opposite. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
